_comment: Seed corpus assembled from kernel.chat's 2026-05-11..18 outreach push. Candidates are organized by the disciplines they care about. Channels are listed preferred-first. Email confidence is 'verified' only where the address has been confirmed delivered or replied; 'medium' for org-convention guesses; 'low' for personal-domain guesses. Templates are reusable across artifacts.

discipline-namer-radar.subject: {artifact_subject} — written for Radar's beat
discipline-namer-radar.body:
{name_first},

Tim's coaching this past week landed on a framing distinction: field vs discipline. We applied it.

Just shipped {artifact_path} ({artifact_license}) — {artifact_subject}.

The shape is the kind of discipline-mapping work Radar has covered seriously for two decades. Offering this either as source material for Radar's own coverage or as a guest piece if that's the right fit.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

discipline-practitioner-unnamed.subject: {artifact_subject} — and you're the obvious namer for one of the open disciplines
discipline-practitioner-unnamed.body:
{name_first},

Just shipped {artifact_path}. The doc maps six disciplines inside agentic engineering. Three are coined and held by kernel.chat. Three remain open to whoever names them first.

Your work sits inside one of the open three. The naming move (and the ROLE.md-style definition that goes with it) is yours if you want it.

Curious whether the field/discipline distinction matches your read.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

discipline-namer-roast.subject: {artifact_subject} — would value a roast if it merits one

discipline-namer-precedent.subject: {artifact_subject} — your framing pattern is the closest precedent
discipline-namer-precedent.body:
{name_first},

Your {role} work is one of the patterns I learned discipline-naming from.

Applied the same shape to agentic engineering. {artifact_subject} ({artifact_license}).

Curious whether the discipline frame reads as serious work to you.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

thought-leader-context.subject: {artifact_subject} — might intersect your work
thought-leader-context.body:
{name_first},

Your work is upstream of where this is going.

Just shipped {artifact_path}.

Curious whether the framing maps to anything in your own taxonomy.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

magazine-peer.subject: {artifact_subject} — would value 60 seconds of your read
magazine-peer.body:
{name_first},

kernel.chat just shipped {artifact_subject}. Your publication is the working model for this kind of positioning move.

One direct question if you have a minute: does the move read as serious work or as a small publication overreaching? I'd rather get the answer now than from silence later.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

hot-take-or-dismiss.subject: {artifact_subject} — would value your read if it merits a hot take
hot-take-or-dismiss.tier: TIER 3 — Optional; fire only if Tier 1 signal is strong
hot-take-or-dismiss.body:
{name_first},

Just shipped {artifact_subject}. You're one of the practitioners I trust to engage seriously OR dismiss decisively. Both are useful; ambivalence isn't.

{artifact_link}

Isaac

candidates.name: Mike Loukides
candidates.notes: Tim O'Reilly's collaborator on Radar; natural escalation post-Tim engagement.

candidates.name: Charity Majors
candidates.notes: Natural namer for the operations / agent-SRE discipline (one of the unnamed three).

candidates.name: Harrison Chase
candidates.notes: Now that orchestration engineering is named and held by kernel.chat, his role shifts from 'natural namer' to 'practitioner whose framework sits in the discipline.'

candidates.name: Linus Lee
candidates.notes: Practitioner in the unnamed skill-curation discipline.

channels.note: verify; email at hashimotos.org bounced 2026-05-18 — try mitchellh.com

candidates.name: Yohei Nakajima
candidates.role: Founder, Untapped Capital / creator of BabyAGI
candidates.notes: Practiced agentic engineering before the field had a name.

candidates.name: Aaron Patterson (tenderlove)
candidates.notes: Email-unreliable; use Bluesky or Mastodon in user's hands.

candidates.name: Bret Taylor
candidates.notes: Sierra is the purest enterprise orchestration play; relevant post-orchestration-engineering naming.

candidates.name: Erik Bernhardsson
candidates.notes: Modal sits adjacent to agent-OS at the runtime layer.

candidates.name: swyx (Shawn Wang)
candidates.role: Latent Space podcast; AI Engineer Summit
candidates.notes: Latent Space is the closest podcast match for the field-map artifact.

candidates.name: Dwarkesh Patel
candidates.notes: Newer podcast; reads pitches more carefully than Lex.

candidates.name: Gergely Orosz
candidates.notes: Reaches senior engineers; field-mapping work is on his beat.

candidates.name: Mike Krieger
candidates.notes: Anthropic CPO; would care about discipline-naming around their product surface.
